zotero.abstractNote: We introduce the Discrete Key-Value Bottleneck (DKVB), a representation learning framework that learns to store information in a discrete, structured memory. DKVB decomposes the input into a set of keys and values, where the keys act as pointers to a learned codebook of values. This bottleneck encourages the model to learn a compact, interpretable, and reusable representation of the data. We demonstrate that DKVB achieves competitive performance on a variety of tasks, including image classification, reinforcement learning, and language modeling, while providing insights into the learned representations.
